content.claim: Hungarian-specific research by Horn and TARKI found that in micro-regions with accessible 8-year gimnázium, socioeconomic status correlates significantly more strongly with competency scores by grade 8 compared to regions without such tracks, suggesting early selection widens SES-linked outcome gaps within same locality over time.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Causal attribution between track availability and SES-outcome divergence not established; selection effects confound observed correlation
Comparison groups (micro-regions with vs. without 8-year track) may differ on unmeasured socioeconomic characteristics
Time period and exact outcome measures not specified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
